blocks.content: Brooke found her at her locker after sixth period.
Daisy saw her coming and had half a second to decide how she was going to be about this. She decided: fine. She closed her locker and waited.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: Hey. You got a minute?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Yeah.
Brooke glanced at the hallway, then back. She wasn't nervous — she was being careful. There was a difference, and Daisy noticed it.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: I didn't know. About that place. What it meant to you guys. Kit never told me.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know he didn't.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: I'm not trying to make excuses for him.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know that too.
Brooke looked at her. Reading something.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: But it was something. For you.
Daisy went still. The hallway moved around them — a locker slamming three rows down, a group of boys too loud near the water fountain. She didn't answer.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: You don't have to say it. I'm not here to make you.
Daisy looked at her. Brooke's expression was clear — not pitying, not triumphant, not anything Daisy would have resented. Just honest.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: He talks about you, you know. Like —
She stopped. Started again.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: Like everything else is the context and you're the subject. I didn't know what to do with that when we first started talking. Now I do.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Brooke —

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: I'm stepping back. That's all I wanted to say. Not because of anything you did. I just know when something's already figured out, even when the people in it haven't caught up yet.
She picked up her bag. Started down the hall. Then stopped and turned once.

blocks.speaker: Brooke
blocks.content: You're really good at keeping it in. For what it's worth.
Daisy stood at her locker for a moment after she was gone. The hallway noise came back all at once. She picked up her bag. Went home.
She didn't call it anything on the walk back. She'd gotten very good at that.
